chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 550 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on MA No.4690/2023 (U/A) 2. For orders on MA No.4691/2023 (E/A) 3. For hearing of bail application 17.08.2023 Mr. Amir Ali Bhutto, Advocate for applicants/accused..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1. Urgent application is granted. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 3. Through instant bail application, applicants/accused Shahnawaz S/o Haji Sultan Ahmed Umrani, Haq Nawaz S/o Sultan Ahmed Umrani, Ali Nawaz S/o Shahnawaz Umrani and Kareem Bux S/o Hussain Bux Joiya seek protective bail in Crime No.38 of 2022 of Police Station Bijli Road, District Quetta, registered for offences under Sections 504, 506/B, 34, P.P.C. Learned Counsel for the applicants/accused contends that the applicants are innocent and have falsely been implicated by the complainant with mala fide intention and ulterior motives. He further contends that the civil litigation is pending adjudication between the parties; however, they were unaware about the registration of the subject FIR. He further contends that the applicants/accused are ready to appear before the Court concerned along with an application under Section 498, Cr.P.C. to prove their innocence, however, they apprehend their immediate arrest as non-bailable warrants have been issued against them; hence, they seek protective bail. Without touching merits and de-merits of the case, protective bail for a period of ten days w.e.f. 17.08.2023 to 26.08.2023 (both days inclusive) is granted to the applicants/accused subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.10,000/- (Rupees ten thousand) each and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of

He further contends that the applicants/accused are ready to appear before the Court concerned along with an application under Section 498, Cr.P.C. to prove their innocence, however, they apprehend their immediate arrest as non-bailable warrants have been issued against them; hence, they seek protective bail. Without touching merits and de-merits of the case, protective bail for a period of ten days w.e.f. 17.08.2023 to 26.08.2023 (both days inclusive) is granted to the applicants/accused subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.10,000/- (Rupees ten thousand) each and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of Cr. Bail App. No. S – 550 of 2023 Page 2 of 2 this Court. Operation of non-bailable warrants in this case shall remain suspended during aforesaid period of ten days. Applicants/accused are directed to surrender before the Court concerned in the aforementioned period. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of above ten days period or whenever the applicants/accused surrender before the Court concerned, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear that in case the applicants/accused fail to surrender before the Court concerned within stipulated period, their bail bonds shall stand automatically forfeited. Criminal Bail Application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Abdul Basit
